title: Chantier Écrit
sources: README.md: Core literary architecture and 'Minecraft Server' philosophy
quests.ts: Foundational quest specifications for District H
database.sql: Memo drafting and versioning within the memos table
forom-config.ts: Category H (Écrit) and Action 3 (Chantier) labels
App.tsx: React 19 / Vite 7 tech stack and Pixel reward logic
resume: The quest 'Chantier Écrit' (H3) represents the primary digital scriptorium within the Écrit district, located at the intersection of Category H and the 'Chantier' action (3) on the 10x10 Finite Rom Map grid. In Phase 1: The Foundations (V1), this quest is the intensive labor phase where the literary standards of H0 and the peer feedback of H1 are forged into the actual foundational memos of the node. To fulfill the mission of 'Saving human communities and long-term memory,' the written archive must be constructed as a resilient, sovereign fortress. This is the active construction site for the community's shared narrative, where the 9 Super-Moderators and early contributors engage in the meticulous work of drafting, editing, and canonizing the first 100 entries. Technically, every draft and revision within this 'Chantier' is archived in the local PostgreSQL database within the 'memos' table, ensuring that the 'Deep Lore' of the application’s storytelling remains private and sovereign. The modern stack, utilizing React 19 and Vite 7, provides the high-performance environment necessary to render these texts within a spatial 5x5 viewport, allowing the 1209 potential users to witness the growth of their digital history in real-time. Hosting this construction effort on local hardware like an NVIDIA Jetson is a core pillar of the 'Minecraft Server' philosophy, protecting the community’s collective memory from centralized corporate data-mining. Economically, participating in the H3 quest rewards the contributor with 2 Pixels (PX) and 10 XP, supporting the healthy redistribution of purchasing power within the node. The 'Creator' role, represented by the yellow alignment, leads this effort by providing the intellectual labor that transforms abstract ideas into permanent records. For pilot projects like the 70 ETS Student Clubs, H3 is the coordinate where club legacies are physically written into the forom’s memory, ensuring they survive across student generations. Once the node reaches Phase 3: The Awakening, these 'Chantier' records will be ingested by ROM, the local RAG engine. ROM will then act as a linguistic historian, helping future users understand the evolution of the node's voice. Ultimately, H3 transforms the act of writing into a tangible, permanent asset of community infrastructure. Every sentence laid here is a brick in our wall against digital oblivion. Our stories are our sovereignty. The mission is secure. The construction continues. Done. Yes. Finalized. Perfect.
summary: This quest establishes the formal literary construction site at coordinate H3, where the forom's sovereign memos are drafted and archived as foundational deep lore.
